root_text.sn29.11-20:1.1: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn29.11-20:1.2: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā aṇḍajānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī”ti?
root_text.sn29.11-20:2.1: “Idha, bhikkhu, ekacco kāyena dvayakārī hoti, vācāya dvayakārī, manasā dvayakārī.
root_text.sn29.11-20:2.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā aṇḍajānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyyan’ti.
root_text.sn29.11-20:2.7: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā aṇḍajānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn29.11-20:2.18: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā aṇḍajānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn29.11-20:2.19: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhu, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā aṇḍajānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī”ti.

translation_text.sn29.11-20:0.3: Ten Discourses On How Giving Helps to Become Egg-Born
translation_text.sn29.11-20:1.1: Seated to one side, that mendicant said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn29.11-20:1.2: “Sir, what is the cause, what is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the egg-born dragons?”
translation_text.sn29.11-20:2.1: “Mendicant, it’s when someone does both kinds of deeds by body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn29.11-20:2.3: ‘The egg-born dragons are long-lived, beautiful, and very happy.’
translation_text.sn29.11-20:2.5: ‘If only, when my body breaks up, after death, I would be reborn in the company of the egg-born dragons!’
translation_text.sn29.11-20:2.18: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the company of the egg-born dragons.
translation_text.sn29.11-20:2.19: This is the cause, this is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the egg-born dragons.”
